response: Founded in 1861, the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) now stands as the nation’s top-ranked university, claiming the #1 spot with an endowment of $24.6 billion. It educates about 11,500 students with the help of 1,069 faculty members, boasts an illustrious roster of 98 Nobel laureates, and—despite a tuition of $57,590 per year—admits only 3.9 percent of its applicants.
